nameMeanings.name: Middleton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places so called. In over thirty instances from many different areas, the name is from Old English midel ‘middle’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. However, Middleton on the Hill near Leominster in Herefordshire appears in Domesday Book as Miceltune, the first element clearly being Old English micel ‘large’, ‘great’ (see Middlebrook ). Middleton Baggot and Middleton Priors in Shropshire have early spellings that suggest gem̄thhyll (from gem̄th ‘confluence’ + hyll ‘hill’) + tūn as the origin. This surname is also found in Scotland and Ireland.

History: A Scottish family of this name derives it from lands at Middleto(u)n near Kincardine. The Scottish physician Peter Middleton practiced in New York City after 1752 and was one of the founders of the medical school at King's College (now Columbia University) in 1767. One of the earliest of the Charleston, SC, Middleton family of prominent legislators was Arthur Middleton, born in Charleston in 1681. The name has also been established in Ireland since at least the 13th century; its presence was reinforced by settlers in the 17th century.

variants.name: Midthun
meanings: Norwegian: habitational name from any of about ten farmsteads in western Norway named Midtun, from Old Norse mith ‘middle’ + tún ‘enclosure, farmstead’. Compare Middleton .

variants.name: Melton
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from any of various places, for example in Leicestershire, Lincolnshire, Norfolk, and Yorkshire, all of which have the same origin as Middleton , with Old English middel replaced by its Old Norse equivalent methal after the Scandinavian settlement of northern and eastern England.

variants.name: Middlebrook
meanings: English: habitational name from a lost Micklebrook in Blackburn (Lancashire), recorded as Mikelbroc in the 13th century. The placename appears to derive from Old English micel ‘big’ + brōc ‘brook, stream’. The surname has undergone a dissimilatory or folk-etymological change from Mickle- to Middle-.

variants.name: Milton
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the many places called Milton, or sometimes Middleton (see Middleton ) and occasionally Melton (see Melton ), most of which derive from Old English middel ‘middle’ or myln ‘mill’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas Middleton was born about 1410, in Middleton, Westmorland, England as the son of John Middleton of Middleton Hall and Elizabeth Metcalfe. He had at least 5 sons and 4 daughters with Isabel Musgrave.

fr.lifeSummary: Thomas Middleton est né environ 1410, à Middleton, Westmorland, Angleterre, fils de John Middleton of Middleton Hall et Elizabeth Metcalfe. Il a eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles avec Isabel Musgrave.

de.lifeSummary: Thomas Middleton wurde um 1410 in Middleton, Westmorland, England, als Sohn von John Middleton of Middleton Hall und Elizabeth Metcalfe geboren. Er hatte mit Isabel Musgrave mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Thomas Middleton è nato il circa 1410 nel Middleton, Westmorland, England come figlio di John Middleton of Middleton Hall e Elizabeth Metcalfe. Lui avuto almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie con Isabel Musgrave.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Middletonは約1410年にイギリスウエストモーランドミッドレトンでJohn Middleton of Middleton HallとElizabeth Metcalfeの息子として生まれた。彼はIsabel Musgraveとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Middleton(은)는 대략 1410년 영국 웨스트모를랜드 미들레턴에서 John Middleton of Middleton Hall(와)과 Elizabeth Metcalfe의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Isabel Musgrave(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Thomas Middleton nasceu aproximadamente 1410, em Middleton, Westmorland, Inglaterra filho de John Middleton of Middleton Hall e Elizabeth Metcalfe. Ele teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas com Isabel Musgrave.

ru.lifeSummary: Thomas Middleton родился около 1410 г. в местности миддлетон, вестморланд, Англия. Его родителей звали John Middleton of Middleton Hall и Elizabeth Metcalfe. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери от Isabel Musgrave.

es.lifeSummary: Thomas Middleton nació aproximadamente 1410, en Middleton, Westmorland, Inglaterra como hijo de John Middleton of Middleton Hall y Elizabeth Metcalfe. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas con Isabel Musgrave.
